notes: Partially supported, see bug 12382004.
Until Safari 6, textPath was not re-rendered when the referenced path was changed dynamically (see bug 15799)
Before version 56, Firefox does not completely follow the use cascading rules (see bug 265894). A fix is documented by Amelia Bellamy-Royds on StackOverflow.
Partially supported, see bug 12382004.
Safari incorrectly interprets clip: auto as clip: rect(auto, auto, auto, auto).
Only the default value of sRGB is implemented
Only the default value of sRGB is implemented
Only the default value of sRGB is implemented
Starting in Firefox 67, the maximum size allowed for custom cursors is 32x32 pixels due to cursors being misused by certain malicious sites.
Before Firefox 40, font-size-adjust: 0 was incorrectly interpreted as font-size-adjust: none (bug 1144885).
A font-stretch definition must be added to the @font-face before this property will function.
Before Firefox 44, oblique was not distinguished from italic.
Before Edge 79, BackgroundImage and BackgroundAlpha were supported.

omit_external_fragment.description: Reference an external document's root element by omitting the fragment.

notes: This property is exposed but has no effect if the browser.send_pings preference is not set to true. See bug 951104.
Partially supported, see bug 12382004.

Until Firefox 10, handling of text-overflow on blocks with inline overflow on both horizontal sides was incorrect. Before Firefox 10, if only one value was specified (such as text-overflow: ellipsis;), text was ellipsed on both sides of the block, instead of only the end edge based on the block's text direction.
This property is only supported on Windows and Linux.
Initial versions had bugs on Windows and Linux that broke font substitution, small-caps, letter-spacing or caused text to overlap. See bug 40156511, bug 40430936, bug 40444347, bug 40286561.
This property is only supported on Windows and Linux.
Initial versions had bugs on Windows and Linux that broke font substitution, small-caps, letter-spacing or caused text to overlap. See bug 40156511, bug 40430936, bug 40444347, bug 40286561.
This property is only supported on Windows and Linux.
The optimizeSpeed option has no effect on Firefox 4 because the standard code for text rendering is already fast and there is not a faster code path at this time. See bug 595688 for details.
Does not work with transform SVG presentation attribute. Only works with transform CSS property. See bug 201854.
Firefox 42 added support for bidirectional and RTL scripts in vertical modes.
Document fragments (including references to fragments in the current document) are not supported (see bug 455986).
